name: Monstera obliqua 'Putumayo Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Putumayo Form' is a highly sought-after botanical treasure hailing from the lush rainforests of the western Amazon basin. This delicate epiphyte is distinguished by its refined, narrow, and thin asymmetric foliage, which features balanced, elongated oval fenestrations. As an inhabitant of the dim understory, it evolved as a light climber that thrives when given the chance to scale mossy surfaces. Its rarity stems from its specific geographical origin in the Putumayo region, setting it apart from other well-known forms like the Peruvian or Zamora-Chinchipe variants.

fieldNotes.title: From the Colombia-Peru Borderlands
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A locality form of Monstera obliqua from the Putumayo region — the disputed and complex border zone between Colombia and Peru, a terrain of tremendous botanical diversity and somewhat complicated access for obvious reasons. Very rare, top price tier. The Putumayo locality is significant because the Colombia-Peru borderlands host plant populations that have been inconsistently collected due to access challenges, meaning the material is genuinely scarce in cultivation beyond typical speculative rarity.
